solution: 
class Solution:
    def answerString(self, word: str, numFriends: int) -> str:
        if numFriends == 1:
            return word
        n = len(word)
        max_len = n - numFriends + 1
        best = ""
        for i in range(n - max_len + 1):
            cand = word[i:i+max_len]
            if cand > best:
                best = cand
        return best
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n^2)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The lexicographically largest substring that can ever appear in the box is the lexicographically largest substring of length (n - numFriends + 1), because any split must leave at least one character in each of the numFriends parts, so the longest possible single part has exactly that length. We scan once to find the maximum such substring.
